findings.evidence: PR line 62 defines `adicOrd`; Mathlib has `FractionalIdeal.count` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/DedekindDomain/Factorization.lean:318`, additivity at lines 372-374, principal ideals via `toPrincipalIdeal` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/ClassGroup/Basic.lean:48`, and factorization reconstruction at `Factorization.lean:568-570`.
findings.fix: Define it through, or at least prove a simp/bridge theorem to, `FractionalIdeal.count K v (toPrincipalIdeal R K u : FractionalIdeal R⁰ K)` so downstream code can reuse Mathlib factorization lemmas.
findings.issue: `adicOrd` reimplements the exponent of a principal fractional ideal from the raw `HeightOneSpectrum.valuation`/`WithZero.log` API instead of connecting to Mathlib’s existing fractional-ideal exponent API.

findings.evidence: PR line 135 defines `DivisorClassGroup`; Mathlib already defines the ideal class group as `ClassGroup R` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/ClassGroup/Basic.lean:86-89`, with quotient map `ClassGroup.mk` at lines 102-105 and `ClassGroup.mk0` at lines 253-255.
findings.fix: Either use `ClassGroup R` for the exported Dedekind-domain class group, or add a canonical equivalence between `(OrderSystem.ofDedekindDomain R K).ClassGroup` and Mathlib’s `ClassGroup R` and name the new abbrev so it is clearly the Weil-divisor presentation.
findings.issue: `DivisorClassGroup R K` is introduced as a second public class-group type for a Dedekind domain, parallel to Mathlib’s existing `ClassGroup R`, with no equivalence or reuse point.

summary: The PR adds the intended TauCeti order-system instance, but it exposes parallel Dedekind divisor/class-group APIs without tying them to Mathlib’s existing fractional-ideal valuation and class-group APIs.
